item.title: France Charges 88 in Crackdown on Crypto Wrench Attacks | French authorities charge 88 individuals, including minors, for violent crypto wrench att... https://Blockchain.News/news/france-charges-88-crypto-wrench-attacks
item.description:
Original title: France Charges 88 in Crackdown on Crypto Wrench Attacks Summary: French authorities charge 88 individuals, including minors, for violent crypto wrench attacks; 75 suspects are in pretrial detention as investigations continue. Article text: French authorities charge 88 individuals, including minors, for violent crypto wrench attacks; 75 suspects are in pretrial detention as investigations continue.
French authorities have charged 88 individuals, including 10 minors, in connection with a wave of violent "wrench attacks" targeting cryptocurrency holders. Of those charged, 75 are being held in pretrial detention, according to Vanessa Perrée, France’s national prosecutor for organized crime. The arrests span 12 cases currently under investigation by specialized judges in the Paris Judicial Court.
Wrench attacks—named after a satirical webcomic highlighting how physical force can bypass digital security—typically involve coercing victims to surrender crypto wallet credentials through methods like home invasions, kidnappings, and extortion. The rise of these attacks reflects a troubling trend in offline security risks tied to digital asset ownership.
France has seen a marked increase in such incidents. According to the National Prosecutor's Office for Organized Crime (PNACO), there were 18 wrench attacks in 2024, 67 in 2025, and 47 already reported in 2026. Global data supports this surge, with blockchain security firm CertiK reporting a 75% uptick in such attacks worldwide in 2025 compared to the previous year.
Investigators believe these crimes are not isolated incidents but the work of structured criminal networks. "These consolidations were made possible through the identification of individuals recurrently involved in multiple cases," Perrée said in a statement. Ongoing investigations aim to uncover financial channels, apprehend additional perpetrators, and dismantle these networks entirely.
As cryptocurrency ownership becomes increasingly visible due to rising asset values—Bitcoin (BTC), for instance, is trading at $64,000 as of April 27, 2026—criminals appear more incentivized to target holders. Blockchain intelligence firm TRM Labs has attributed the rise in attacks to the perceived pseudonymity of crypto transactions and the ease with which personal data can be obtained online.
Perrée urged crypto holders to adopt stricter personal security measures, emphasizing the dangers of "overexposure on social networks that could make them targets." Publicly sharing crypto holdings, for example, can provide attackers with the information they need to identify high-value victims.
Telegram founder Pavel Durov recently suggested that some of these attacks in France may have been fueled by the misuse of crypto investors' tax data, allegedly leaked by a former tax official. While authorities have not confirmed this, it underscores the broader challenges of protecting personal information in a digital age.
For crypto holders, the risks extend far beyond online scams or exchange hacks. Wrench attacks demonstrate how physical security must be part of any robust crypto asset protection strategy. Avoiding unnecessary public disclosure and implementing multi-layered security practices, such as hardware wallets stored in secure locations, can reduce exposure to these threats.
With 88 individuals already charged and investigations ongoing, French authorities appear committed to curbing this alarming trend. However, as crypto values continue to rise, so too does the incentive for criminals to exploit the industry’s vulnerabilities, both digital and physical.

item.title: Google DeepMind Partners with South Korea for AI Campus | DeepMind teams up with Korea's MSIT to launch an AI Campus focused on scientific breakthr... https://Blockchain.News/news/google-deepmind-partners-south-korea-ai-campus
item.description:
Original title: Google DeepMind Partners with South Korea for AI Campus Summary: DeepMind teams up with Korea's MSIT to launch an AI Campus focused on scientific breakthroughs, AI safety, and talent development. Article text: DeepMind teams up with Korea's MSIT to launch an AI Campus focused on scientific breakthroughs, AI safety, and talent development.
Google DeepMind has announced a partnership with South Korea’s Ministry of Science and ICT (MSIT) to establish an AI Campus in Seoul. This collaboration aims to accelerate scientific breakthroughs, nurture local AI talent, and explore advancements in AI safety research. The partnership is part of Korea’s broader K-Moonshot Missions initiative, which focuses on addressing national scientific challenges and boosting research productivity.
The planned AI Campus will serve as a hub for collaboration between Korean research institutions and Google’s AI experts. Initial partnerships include Seoul National University (SNU), Korea Advanced Institute of Science and Technology (KAIST), and the Ministry’s AI Bio Innovation Hubs. Together, they will leverage DeepMind’s cutting-edge AI technologies across fields such as life sciences, energy, weather, and climate science.
In addition to research, the partnership aims to foster AI talent in Korea. Google DeepMind plans to create internship opportunities for Korean students, building on Google’s recent initiative to provide 50,000 AI Essentials scholarships in the region. This aligns with Korea’s growing investment in AI infrastructure, including the upcoming launch of the National AI for Science Center (NAIS) in May 2026.
AI safety is also a cornerstone of the partnership. Following Google’s 2024 Frontier AI Safety Commitments at the AI Seoul Summit, DeepMind will collaborate with the Korean AI Safety Institute (AISI) on best practices and research to ensure responsible development and deployment of AI technologies.
This move builds on DeepMind’s legacy in Korea, which began with the highly publicized AlphaGo victory over Go champion Lee Sedol in 2016. The new partnership underscores DeepMind’s ambitions to remain a global leader in AI while contributing to scientific and societal progress.
For investors tracking Alphabet Inc. (NASDAQ: GOOGL), DeepMind’s parent company, this partnership highlights the tech giant’s focus on AI-driven innovation and its expanding footprint in Asia. While direct financial implications remain unclear, the strategic importance of AI leadership positions Alphabet well in an increasingly competitive sector.

item.title: Prediction Markets Driven by 3.5% of Users, Study Finds | New research reveals that an 'informed minority' drives price accuracy in prediction mark... https://Blockchain.News/news/prediction-markets-informed-minority-study
item.description:
Original title: Prediction Markets Driven by 3.5% of Users, Study Finds Summary: New research reveals that an 'informed minority' drives price accuracy in prediction markets, raising questions about fairness and insider trading risks. Article text: New research reveals that an 'informed minority' drives price accuracy in prediction markets, raising questions about fairness and insider trading risks.
Prediction markets, often touted as a tool for crowd-sourced forecasting, are dominated by a small, highly informed group of traders, according to a study by researchers from London Business School and Yale University. The study analyzed Polymarket data from 2023 to 2025 and found that just 3.5% of accounts generate the bulk of price discovery, while the majority of participants fund this accuracy through losses.
The findings challenge the perception that prediction markets operate on the "wisdom of the crowd." Instead, the authors argue, their accuracy reflects the "wisdom of an informed minority"—market makers and skilled traders who earn outsized profits. On average, market maker accounts pocketed $11,830 each during the study period, while other informed traders collectively captured over 30% of total gains. The majority of participants, labeled as "unlucky losers," covered aggregate losses.
Prediction markets have become a hotbed of activity in the crypto sector, recording over $15 billion in monthly trading volume as of 2026. Platforms like Polymarket and Kalshi allow users to trade contracts on events ranging from elections to sports outcomes, offering a financial incentive to aggregate collective beliefs.
However, this rapid growth has drawn regulatory scrutiny. Concerns over insider trading are mounting, with critics arguing that the pseudonymous nature of these platforms makes them ripe for abuse. A recent case on April 26 involved a soldier allegedly using classified information to profit on a prediction market bet. Researchers noted that prediction markets face less oversight compared to securities markets, opening the door for private information to influence trading outcomes.
The study's findings highlight a potential ethical quandary: while the informed minority drives market efficiency, their gains come at the expense of less-informed participants. This dynamic raises questions about fairness, particularly as platforms gain traction among a broader user base. Regulatory pressures are likely to increase, with platforms potentially needing to adopt stricter rules to mitigate insider trading risks. For instance, some platforms have begun banning political candidates from betting on their own campaigns.
The study also underscores the profitability gap within prediction markets. Another analysis, released earlier this month, found that only 0.015% of traders on Polymarket achieved consistent profits of $5,000 or more over four consecutive months. These findings suggest that prediction markets, while lucrative for a select few, offer limited opportunities for the average user to turn a profit.
As prediction markets evolve, the pressure to address transparency and fairness will grow. Kalshi and Polymarket's recent plans to introduce perpetual contracts signal innovation, but they may also invite further regulatory oversight. With trading volumes soaring and insider trading concerns escalating, the next few years will likely define the long-term viability of these platforms.
For now, traders should approach prediction markets with caution, understanding that the odds may be tilted in favor of an informed minority who dominate price discovery and profitability.

item.content:encoded:
New research reveals that an 'informed minority' drives price accuracy in prediction markets, raising questions about fairness and insider trading risks.
Prediction markets, often touted as a tool for crowd-sourced forecasting, are dominated by a small, highly informed group of traders, according to a study by researchers from London Business School and Yale University. The study analyzed Polymarket data from 2023 to 2025 and found that just 3.5% of accounts generate the bulk of price discovery, while the majority of participants fund this accuracy through losses.
The findings challenge the perception that prediction markets operate on the "wisdom of the crowd." Instead, the authors argue, their accuracy reflects the "wisdom of an informed minority"—market makers and skilled traders who earn outsized profits. On average, market maker accounts pocketed $11,830 each during the study period, while other informed traders collectively captured over 30% of total gains. The majority of participants, labeled as "unlucky losers," covered aggregate losses.
Prediction markets have become a hotbed of activity in the crypto sector, recording over $15 billion in monthly trading volume as of 2026. Platforms like Polymarket and Kalshi allow users to trade contracts on events ranging from elections to sports outcomes, offering a financial incentive to aggregate collective beliefs.
However, this rapid growth has drawn regulatory scrutiny. Concerns over insider trading are mounting, with critics arguing that the pseudonymous nature of these platforms makes them ripe for abuse. A recent case on April 26 involved a soldier allegedly using classified information to profit on a prediction market bet. Researchers noted that prediction markets face less oversight compared to securities markets, opening the door for private information to influence trading outcomes.
The study's findings highlight a potential ethical quandary: while the informed minority drives market efficiency, their gains come at the expense of less-informed participants. This dynamic raises questions about fairness, particularly as platforms gain traction among a broader user base. Regulatory pressures are likely to increase, with platforms potentially needing to adopt stricter rules to mitigate insider trading risks. For instance, some platforms have begun banning political candidates from betting on their own campaigns.
The study also underscores the profitability gap within prediction markets. Another analysis, released earlier this month, found that only 0.015% of traders on Polymarket achieved consistent profits of $5,000 or more over four consecutive months. These findings suggest that prediction markets, while lucrative for a select few, offer limited opportunities for the average user to turn a profit.
As prediction markets evolve, the pressure to address transparency and fairness will grow. Kalshi and Polymarket's recent plans to introduce perpetual contracts signal innovation, but they may also invite further regulatory oversight. With trading volumes soaring and insider trading concerns escalating, the next few years will likely define the long-term viability of these platforms.
For now, traders should approach prediction markets with caution, understanding that the odds may be tilted in favor of an informed minority who dominate price discovery and profitability.

item.title: Senator Tillis Drops Opposition to Warsh Fed Chair Nomination | Senator Thom Tillis ends opposition to Kevin Warsh's Fed chair confirmation following DOJ... https://Blockchain.News/news/tillis-ends-warsh-fed-chair-block
item.description:
Original title: Senator Tillis Drops Opposition to Warsh Fed Chair Nomination Summary: Senator Thom Tillis ends opposition to Kevin Warsh's Fed chair confirmation following DOJ's closure of Powell probe. Senate vote set for April 29. Article text: Senator Thom Tillis ends opposition to Kevin Warsh's Fed chair confirmation following DOJ's closure of Powell probe. Senate vote set for April 29.
Republican Senator Thom Tillis announced he will no longer oppose Kevin Warsh’s nomination as Federal Reserve chair, clearing a key hurdle for Warsh ahead of the Senate Banking Committee's scheduled vote on April 29. Tillis reversed his stance after the Department of Justice (DOJ) concluded its investigation into current Fed Chair Jerome Powell's handling of a billion-dollar headquarters renovation project.
In a post on X (formerly Twitter), Tillis said the DOJ’s probe had posed “a serious threat to the Fed’s independence.” With the investigation now closed, Tillis expressed confidence in Warsh, stating he is “looking forward” to supporting the nomination. The senator had previously leveraged procedural holds to stall Warsh’s confirmation process.
Powell’s term ends on May 15, with Warsh expected to assume office shortly after a full Senate vote—likely during the week of May 11. Warsh, a former Fed governor, has been described as hawkish on fiscal policy, particularly regarding interest rates. Market watchers, including those in the crypto sector, have debated the potential implications of his monetary stance.
Cryptocurrency investors may find Warsh’s leadership pivotal. Historically, hawkish Fed policies—such as higher interest rates—tend to suppress risk assets, including digital currencies. At the same time, Warsh has shown a nuanced interest in crypto. According to recent investment disclosures, Warsh holds stakes in over 30 blockchain projects, including Solana and decentralized exchange dYdX. This could signal a more open-minded approach to crypto regulation compared to his predecessors.
Despite speculation about his policy direction, Warsh has indicated that his decisions will remain independent, dismissing any suggestion of political pressure from former President Donald Trump, who has repeatedly called for rate cuts. Warsh’s confirmation could mark a departure from Powell’s tenure, but his actual monetary policy stance remains uncertain and could have significant implications for both traditional and crypto markets.
The Senate Banking Committee’s vote this week will determine whether Warsh’s nomination advances to the full Senate floor, setting the stage for a leadership transition that could reverberate across financial markets.

item.title: Aave Pushes Arbitrum to Release Frozen $73M ETH Post-Kelp Hack | Aave urges Arbitrum to unfreeze $73M in stolen ETH, aiming to restore rsETH backing after... https://Blockchain.News/news/aave-arbitrum-unfreeze-eth-kelp-dao-hack
item.description:
Original title: Aave Pushes Arbitrum to Release Frozen $73M ETH Post-Kelp Hack Summary: Aave urges Arbitrum to unfreeze $73M in stolen ETH, aiming to restore rsETH backing after the $292M Kelp DAO hack. Article text: Aave urges Arbitrum to unfreeze $73M in stolen ETH, aiming to restore rsETH backing after the $292M Kelp DAO hack.
Aave Labs has formally requested that Arbitrum's decentralized governance unfreeze $73.5 million in Ether (ETH) tied to the April 18 Kelp DAO exploit. The funds, currently locked by the Arbitrum Security Council, are intended to be redirected to “DeFi United,” a recently established recovery fund aimed at compensating holders of rsETH, the hacked restaked Ether token.
The Kelp DAO hack, which drained $292 million in rsETH, exploited vulnerabilities in the DAO's cross-chain bridge infrastructure. Attackers, reportedly linked to North Korea's Lazarus Group, exploited weak verifier configurations to manipulate the bridge and steal funds. A portion of the stolen ETH—30,765 ETH—was frozen by Arbitrum on April 21 to prevent further laundering and damage to the ecosystem.
In a governance proposal posted on April 26, Aave Labs outlined a plan for the frozen ETH to be sent to a recovery address co-managed by Aave, Kelp DAO, and security platform Certora. DeFi United, the recovery fund launched just days ago, has already secured $21 million in pledges from contributors like Aave Labs CEO Stani Kulechov, LayerZero, and the Golem Foundation. If approved, the funds would help restore rsETH’s 1:1 backing and support liquidity providers and borrowers impacted by the hack.
Additionally, larger pledges—totaling $215 million—have been made by Arbitrum, Mantle, and Lido, though these are subject to governance votes. Aave Labs emphasized that even a partial recovery would significantly reduce the shortfall caused by the hack. They’ve set a seven-week timeline for the recovery process, promising to return unused funds if the effort falls short.
The Kelp DAO hack sent shockwaves through the DeFi sector. Aave, one of the hardest-hit protocols, saw its total value locked (TVL) drop nearly $12 billion within a week after the attacker used stolen rsETH as collateral to borrow wrapped ETH, leaving Aave with $190 million in bad debt. Activity across Arbitrum and other impacted platforms has been hampered as liquidity providers and market participants reassess risk.
ETH prices, however, appear resilient. As of April 27, ETH is trading at $2,360.33, up 1.71% in the past 24 hours. The broader market seems largely unaffected, indicating market participants are treating the hack as an isolated event rather than a systemic failure of DeFi infrastructure.
Aave’s proposal now awaits a governance vote within the Arbitrum ecosystem. If approved, DeFi United would gain access to the frozen ETH, accelerating the recovery process. The decision could set a precedent for how decentralized organizations respond to sophisticated exploits and balance security with restorative measures.
With a tight timeline and significant sums at stake, the coming weeks will be critical for Kelp DAO, Aave, and the broader DeFi market. Traders and stakeholders will be watching for the governance outcome and its impact on ecosystem trust.

item.title: Bitcoin Bottom Predicted at $57K by October 2026: Analyst | Crypto investor Michael Terpin forecasts Bitcoin's bottom at $57K in late 2026, citing hi... https://Blockchain.News/news/bitcoin-bottom-57k-october-2026
item.description:
Original title: Bitcoin Bottom Predicted at $57K by October 2026: Analyst Summary: Crypto investor Michael Terpin forecasts Bitcoin's bottom at $57K in late 2026, citing historical price cycles and macroeconomic pressures. Article text: Crypto investor Michael Terpin forecasts Bitcoin's bottom at $57K in late 2026, citing historical price cycles and macroeconomic pressures.
Bitcoin's (BTC) price could see further downside, bottoming out around $57,000 by October 2026, according to crypto investor and author Michael Terpin. The prediction, shared with Cointelegraph, is based on historical price patterns that suggest a typical market cycle drawdown of about one year following a peak.
Terpin pointed to Bitcoin's last all-time high of over $126,000 in October 2025 as the market cycle top. He expects the current downturn to continue until BTC finds support near $57,000, a level that aligns with previous cycle bottoms.
Bitcoin is currently trading at approximately $77,987, following a 29% rally from its February low of $60,000. However, analysts warn that the ongoing rally could be a "fake out." For the next bull run to materialize, Terpin argues that Bitcoin must reclaim the $100,000 threshold, a psychological and technical milestone. He notes that achieving this would require strong ETF inflows and sustained buying from major players like Michael Saylor’s MicroStrategy, combined with stable macroeconomic conditions.
"There's certainly a chance of $100,000 this year, but it's unlikely without a confluence of supportive factors," Terpin added.
Bitcoin's current price action is influenced by broader economic forces, including geopolitical risks and tight liquidity conditions. The war in Iran, volatile oil prices, and unchanged U.S. Federal Reserve interest rates have created a challenging environment for risk assets.
Further complicating the outlook, Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell is set to oversee his final Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meeting this week, with traders overwhelmingly expecting no change in interest rates. "The rate decision is almost certainly a hold flat," remarked market analyst Nic Puckrin.
Crypto market analysts, including Matthew Hyland, remain cautious about Bitcoin's short-term trajectory. Hyland noted a lack of "euphoria or interest" from investors, suggesting skepticism about the rally's sustainability. He anticipates another leg down for BTC by October, potentially to levels as low as $73,000.
Meanwhile, technical indicators like the 21-week exponential moving average (EMA) continue to act as resistance. If this trend persists, Bitcoin could retrace further, with some projections pointing to a short-term support zone around $65,710, according to analyst Rekt Capital.
While Bitcoin's long-term potential remains a focal point for investors, the road to recovery could be prolonged. Traders should closely monitor critical support levels and macroeconomic developments as the year unfolds.

Meanwhile, technical indicators like the 21-week exponential moving average (EMA) continue to act as resistance. If this trend persists, Bitcoin could retrace further, with some projections pointing to a short-term support zone around $65,710, according to analyst Rekt Capital.
While Bitcoin's long-term potential remains a focal point for investors, the road to recovery could be prolonged. Traders should closely monitor critical support levels and macroeconomic developments as the year unfolds.

item.title: LTC Price Prediction: $57 Breakout or $54 Retest Within Two Weeks | Litecoin hovers at $56.22 with momentum fading at resistance. Technical setup suggests 65... https://Blockchain.News/news/20260426-price-prediction-ltc-57-breakout-or-54-retest-within
item.description:
Original title: LTC Price Prediction: $57 Breakout or $54 Retest Within Two Weeks Summary: Litecoin hovers at $56.22 with momentum fading at resistance. Technical setup suggests 65% chance of pullback to $54-55 support before any sustainable move toward $60. Article text: Litecoin hovers at $56.22 with momentum fading at resistance. Technical setup suggests 65% chance of pullback to $54-55 support before any sustainable move toward $60.
Litecoin sits at $56.22 in a delicate balancing act between buyers defending recent gains and sellers stepping in at resistance. The price action reveals a market caught between competing forces - momentum indicators show buyers haven't given up, but they're not pushing aggressively either.
Trading at 78% of the way toward the upper Bollinger Band at $57, LTC appears to be bumping against a ceiling that's proven stubborn. While the coin holds above its short-term moving averages, that positioning alone isn't enough to guarantee upside when the broader trend remains uncertain. The gap to the 200-day moving average at $72.14 underscores how far Litecoin has fallen from previous highs.
The derivatives market tells a story of internal tension despite surface-level stability. Retail positioning skews heavily long at 71.3%, with institutional traders even more bullish at 76.1%. Yet beneath these positioning metrics, actual trading flow paints a different picture entirely.
Recent sessions show aggressive selling outpacing buying by nearly 2:1, based on the taker buy/sell ratio of 0.6046. This disconnect suggests long holders may be quietly distributing positions into any strength rather than adding to bullish bets. Open interest dropped 1.63% over 24 hours while price barely moved - typically a sign of position unwinding rather than fresh conviction entering the market.
The $7.57 million in spot volume provides decent liquidity but falls short of the explosive activity needed to power through established resistance levels. With funding rates slightly negative at -0.0014%, there's no premium being paid for long positions, indicating traders aren't expecting immediate upside despite their positioning.
The current setup resembles a classic consolidation phase where neither buyers nor sellers have gained decisive control. Resistance at $57 has held firm through multiple tests, while support in the $55.78-55.33 zone continues to attract buying interest on any dips.
Blockchain.news analysis suggests this sideways action could persist until either volume surges dramatically or broader crypto market conditions shift. The technical picture shows a market treading water rather than building momentum in either direction.
Hourly candlesticks (about 96 bars), same endpoint as our cryptocurrency price pages. Numbers below refresh from 1-minute klines.
The next two weeks will determine whether Litecoin can break free from its current range or faces another leg down. Based on current momentum patterns and selling pressure, there's a 65% probability LTC tests the $54-55 support zone before attempting any sustained rally.
For bulls to take control, they need to push volume above 15 million daily and drive price cleanly through $57. That scenario carries 35% probability and would open the door to $58-60 within three weeks. However, such a move would require either broader market strength or Litecoin-specific catalysts that aren't currently visible.
The critical level remains $57 resistance. A break below $55 likely sends LTC toward the $53.50 lower Bollinger Band, while a convincing push above $57.50 could target $60 by mid-May. Until this consolidation resolves, risk/reward favors waiting for a clear directional break rather than forcing trades in either direction.

item.title: ATOM Price Prediction: $2.20 Target Despite Smart Money Long Squeeze Setting Up | ATOM's breakout above all major moving averages signals momentum, but aggressive selling... https://Blockchain.News/news/20260426-price-prediction-target-atom-220-despite-smart-...
item.description:
Original title: ATOM Price Prediction: $2.20 Target Despite Smart Money Long Squeeze Setting Up Summary: ATOM's breakout above all major moving averages signals momentum, but aggressive selling pressure and overbought conditions point to a pullback before the next leg up. 65% probability of hitting $2 Article text: ATOM's breakout above all major moving averages signals momentum, but aggressive selling pressure and overbought conditions point to a pullback before the next leg up. 65% probability of hitting $2...
Cosmos is riding a technical breakout wave that's been building since early April. Trading at $2.02, ATOM has decisively cleared every meaningful moving average - the 7-day ($1.92), 20-day ($1.83), and 50-day ($1.80) - while sitting well below the 200-day at $2.27. This positioning screams of a token in transition, breaking out of a prolonged consolidation but still facing the overhead supply from longer-term holders.
The Bollinger Band position at 1.04 tells the immediate story - ATOM is pressing against the upper boundary with conviction, suggesting institutional accumulation has been quietly building. However, the 24-hour volume of just $2 million on Binance spot reveals this isn't retail FOMO driving the move; it's methodical positioning ahead of something bigger.
The technicals are painting a mixed but ultimately bullish picture. RSI at 69.28 sits in that dangerous neutral-to-overbought zone where momentum can either accelerate or stall hard. What's more telling is the MACD histogram flatlined at zero - momentum has peaked for now, but the crossover signals remain intact.
Hourly candlesticks (about 96 bars), same endpoint as our cryptocurrency price pages. Numbers below refresh from 1-minute klines.
The real concern comes from derivatives data where aggressive selling pressure dominates with a taker buy/sell ratio of just 0.83. Someone is methodically distributing into this strength, creating the setup for a healthy pullback. The analysts at Blockchain.news have been tracking similar patterns across the cosmos ecosystem, noting how these technical retracements often precede the strongest rallies.
Smart money positioning reveals the true story. Top traders maintain a 1.59 long/short ratio (61.4% long), showing conviction despite the recent selling pressure. Open interest dropped 2.17% to $22.1 million, suggesting position trimming rather than capitulation - classic profit-taking behavior after a sustained move.
The funding rate at 0.0012% remains neutral, indicating no excessive speculation in either direction. This is exactly the environment where unexpected moves happen. With retail heavily long at 59.1%, any sharp dip will likely trigger cascading liquidations, creating the perfect buying opportunity for institutions.
The bull case hinges on ATOM holding the $1.97 strong support level. A successful defense here opens the door to $2.20 within 10 days, representing the next Fibonacci resistance cluster. The path higher becomes cleaner if volume picks up above the current anemic levels.
The bear case triggers if ATOM breaks below $2.00 psychological support on volume. This would likely send price back to test the $1.83 middle Bollinger Band, potentially creating a double-bottom setup for the next major leg up. Given the current positioning, I assign a 65% probability to the $2.20 target being hit first, with a 35% chance of the $1.83 retest scenario playing out.
The key inflection point comes at $2.05 immediate resistance - clear this level decisively and the momentum trade accelerates toward $2.20. Fail here, and the pullback to $1.97 becomes inevitable.
ATOM's breakout above all major moving averages signals momentum, but aggressive selling pressure and overbought conditions point to a pullback before the next leg up. 65% probability of hitting $2...

item.title: BCH Price Prediction: $720 Still Achievable by Q3 Despite Range-Bound Action | Bitcoin Cash consolidates at $453 while building a foundation for the next major move. Te... https://Blockchain.News/news/20260426-price-prediction-bch-720-still-achievable-by-q3-d...
item.description:
Original title: BCH Price Prediction: $720 Still Achievable by Q3 Despite Range-Bound Action Summary: Bitcoin Cash consolidates at $453 while building a foundation for the next major move. Technical setup suggests breakout potential toward $720 remains intact through Q3 2026. Article text: Bitcoin Cash consolidates at $453 while building a foundation for the next major move. Technical setup suggests breakout potential toward $720 remains intact through Q3 2026.
Bitcoin Cash has settled into accumulation mode at $453, trading in a compressed range that's frustrating momentum traders but rewarding patient capital. The current price sits 13% below the 200-day moving average at $522, creating a clear roadmap for the next directional move.
This sideways action isn't market apathy - it's smart money positioning. While retail attention flows toward trending narratives, BCH quietly absorbs selling pressure and establishes support. The analysts at Blockchain.news view this consolidation as constructive base-building rather than weakness, particularly given the strong support holding above $440.
The indicator alignment tells a story of coiled energy waiting for release. RSI maintains neutral territory around 51, avoiding the extremes that typically precede exhaustion moves. More importantly, the momentum oscillators show no signs of bearish divergence despite the sideways grind.
BCH's position within its Bollinger Bands reveals controlled volatility compression. This type of range contraction historically precedes significant directional breaks, with the current setup favoring upside resolution given the support structure below $440.
Hourly candlesticks (about 96 bars), same endpoint as our cryptocurrency price pages. Numbers below refresh from 1-minute klines.
Volume patterns show institutional accumulation rather than retail distribution. The 24-hour trading volume of $2.87 million reflects steady absorption rather than panic selling, while derivatives positioning reveals a 1.26 long/short ratio among top traders - suggesting professional money expects higher prices.
The bullish scenario unfolds through a decisive break above $458 resistance, which opens direct access to challenge the 200-day moving average at $522. Success at that level historically triggers momentum acceleration in BCH, with $720 representing the next major fibonacci extension.
This target isn't arbitrary speculation. BCH's previous cycles show consistent 60-80% moves once momentum shifts from accumulation to distribution phase. From current levels, $720 represents a 59% advance that aligns with historical precedent and technical projections.
Risk management remains crucial below $440 support. A breakdown through this level would signal extended consolidation, potentially delaying the $720 objective into Q4 2026 rather than invalidating it entirely.
The probability-weighted outcome favors patient bulls willing to accumulate weakness while maintaining defensive stops below key support. BCH's technical structure remains constructive despite the sideways price action, with catalysts likely to emerge as broader crypto markets establish their next directional phase.

item.title: UNI Price Prediction: Brief Rally to $3.45 Before $3.20 Target | UNI trades at $3.29 with technical conditions pointing toward a short-term bounce to $3.4... https://Blockchain.News/news/20260426-uni-price-prediction-brief-rally-to-345-before-320-target
item.description:
Original title: UNI Price Prediction: Brief Rally to $3.45 Before $3.20 Target Summary: UNI trades at $3.29 with technical conditions pointing toward a short-term bounce to $3.45 before momentum weakness drives the token toward $3.20 support over the next week. Article text: UNI trades at $3.29 with technical conditions pointing toward a short-term bounce to $3.45 before momentum weakness drives the token toward $3.20 support over the next week.
Uniswap trades in limbo at $3.29, caught between weak buying interest and exhausted selling pressure. The token sits 33% below its 200-day moving average while daily volume on Binance remains subdued at $4 million - signaling neither bulls nor bears have strong conviction at current levels.
The broader DeFi sector continues struggling as institutional capital flows toward established Layer 1 networks rather than infrastructure tokens. This shift in preference has kept UNI range-bound near multi-month lows despite the protocol's continued dominance in decentralized exchange volume.
UNI's current position suggests a mechanical relief rally is building. The token trades at the middle of its recent range with oversold conditions slowly unwinding, creating space for momentum to build toward overhead resistance.
The key level to watch is $3.45, which aligns with the upper boundary of UNI's recent trading range. This represents the most likely target for any near-term strength as short covering and opportunistic buying could drive a 4.9% move higher from current levels.
However, the longer-term picture remains challenging. UNI continues trading below key moving averages with volume patterns suggesting distribution rather than accumulation among larger holders.
Smart money positioning shows mixed signals in UNI markets. Large holders maintain a slight bullish bias with long positions outweighing shorts, but this apparent optimism is tempered by consistent selling pressure during any price strength.
The derivatives market reflects this uncertainty, with open interest holding steady at $56.8 million and funding rates remaining neutral. Neither side is placing aggressive directional bets, suggesting traders are waiting for clearer signals before committing significant capital.
According to analysts at Blockchain.news, this type of sideways action typically resolves with a brief counter-trend move before the primary trend reasserts itself.
The most probable scenario involves UNI reaching $3.45 within 3-5 sessions as technical conditions support a short-term bounce. This move would trap late buyers before selling pressure returns to drive the token toward $3.20 support.
Risk management becomes crucial at these levels. Any daily close below $3.24 would signal the bounce has failed and open the path to $3.20 over the following 7-10 days. Conversely, a sustained break above $3.50 with increased volume would invalidate the bearish outlook and suggest stronger buying interest has emerged.
Hourly candlesticks (about 96 bars), same endpoint as our cryptocurrency price pages. Numbers below refresh from 1-minute klines.
The current setup favors patience rather than aggressive positioning, with the most attractive risk-reward coming from selling any strength above $3.40 while targeting the $3.20 support zone for potential long entries.

Smart money positioning shows mixed signals in UNI markets. Large holders maintain a slight bullish bias with long positions outweighing shorts, but this apparent optimism is tempered by consistent selling pressure during any price strength. item.title: LINK Price Prediction: $14.50 Target Within Reach as Whales Defy Retail Exodus | LINK trades at $9.43 with institutional money flowing in at 2.52:1 long ratio while retai... https://Blockchain.News/news/20260426-price-prediction-target-link-1450-within-reach-...
item.description:
Original title: LINK Price Prediction: $14.50 Target Within Reach as Whales Defy Retail Exodus Summary: LINK trades at $9.43 with institutional money flowing in at 2.52:1 long ratio while retail traders dump positions. Technical breakout above $9.56 resistance unlocks direct path to $14.50, deliverin Article text: LINK trades at $9.43 with institutional money flowing in at 2.52:1 long ratio while retail traders dump positions. Technical breakout above $9.56 resistance unlocks direct path to $14.50, deliverin...
LINK sits at a crossroads at $9.43, trading 21% below its 200-day moving average while institutional traders quietly build positions. The 2.52:1 long-to-short ratio among professional traders tells the real story – smart money is accumulating while retail hands shake out.
The divergence is stark. Retail traders are aggressively selling with a 0.88 taker buy-sell ratio, yet whales continue loading up on LINK's oracle infrastructure play. This classic setup has preceded significant moves in both directions, but the weight of institutional positioning tips the scales bullish. The analysts at Blockchain.news have identified this accumulation pattern as the primary catalyst driving toward higher price targets.
The chart structure backs the whale thesis. LINK has consolidated between $9.28-$9.45 for weeks, building energy beneath key resistance at $9.56. Breaking this level opens direct runway to the $10.50-$11.00 zone where real selling pressure awaits.
RSI holds steady at 55.16 with room to run, while MACD histogram sits at zero – neutral territory that typically precedes directional moves. The Bollinger Bands position at 0.72 shows LINK in the upper portion of its recent range with space to reach the $9.68 upper band before encountering technical resistance.
Short-term momentum has shifted positive with the 7-day moving average crossing above the 20-day, creating the foundation for sustained upward movement once resistance breaks.
The path to $14.50 becomes clear once LINK clears $9.56 resistance. This level has capped three separate rally attempts over the past month, making a decisive break significant for momentum traders. Success here triggers buying from algorithms programmed to chase breakouts, adding fuel to institutional accumulation.
Open interest has climbed to $85.6 million, indicating growing institutional participation that provides the capital base for major moves. With funding rates neutral at 0.001%, there's no immediate pressure from leveraged positions that could derail a rally.
Hourly candlesticks (about 96 bars), same endpoint as our cryptocurrency price pages. Numbers below refresh from 1-minute klines.
The 53% gain to $14.50 aligns with LINK's historical tendency to move in sharp bursts rather than gradual climbs. Oracle tokens trade on adoption cycles, and LINK's continued integration across DeFi protocols supports higher valuations during risk-on periods.
Failure changes everything. A breakdown below $9.32 support exposes the $8.78 level where Bollinger Band support sits. This scenario invalidates the accumulation thesis and likely triggers stops from recent buyers, creating a cascade toward deeper support levels.
The current setup favors bulls, but crypto markets punish overconfidence. LINK's $0.37 average true range suggests volatility remains elevated, making position sizing and stop placement critical for managing downside risk.
With technical indicators aligned and whale positioning supportive, LINK appears ready for its next major move. The 53% upside to $14.50 offers compelling risk-reward for traders willing to bet on institutional conviction over retail sentiment.

item.title: AVAX Price Prediction: $10.50 Breakout Imminent as Whales Position for 11% Rally | AVAX compressed at $9.45 with whales holding 68.5% long positions sets up a clean breakou... https://Blockchain.News/news/20260426-price-prediction-avax-1050-breakout-imminent-...
item.description:
Original title: AVAX Price Prediction: $10.50 Breakout Imminent as Whales Position for 11% Rally Summary: AVAX compressed at $9.45 with whales holding 68.5% long positions sets up a clean breakout above $9.60 resistance toward $10.50 within two weeks. Technical indicators align bullishly while institut Article text: AVAX compressed at $9.45 with whales holding 68.5% long positions sets up a clean breakout above $9.60 resistance toward $10.50 within two weeks. Technical indicators align bullishly while institut...
AVAX sits at $9.45 in a textbook compression pattern that screams breakout. The daily trading range has contracted to just $0.40 while price holds steady above the 7, 20, and 50-period moving averages clustered between $9.33-$9.37. This isn't consolidation - it's accumulation. When assets compress this tightly above key support levels, the resulting moves typically deliver 8-12% gains within weeks.
The analysts at Blockchain.news have identified this exact setup as a high-probability breakout scenario. RSI at 52.54 provides perfect momentum positioning - strong enough to fuel upside without triggering overbought selling pressure. MACD histogram sits flat at zero, coiling for directional expansion.
Derivatives data reveals the real narrative. Top traders maintain aggressive 2.18:1 long-to-short positioning with 68.5% betting on higher prices. Retail follows at 63% long, creating unusual alignment between smart money and crowd sentiment. When whales position this heavily while technicals compress, breakouts become inevitable rather than probable.
The taker buy/sell ratio at 0.82 shows some distribution, but this appears tactical rather than strategic - whales taking profits on smaller positions while maintaining core longs. Bollinger Band positioning at 0.60 gives AVAX clear runway to the upper band at $9.75 before hitting any technical resistance.
Hourly candlesticks (about 96 bars), same endpoint as our cryptocurrency price pages. Numbers below refresh from 1-minute klines.
The path higher requires breaking $9.60 resistance - the critical level that separates consolidation from acceleration. Once cleared, momentum algorithms trigger buying that typically carries assets to the next major resistance zone. For AVAX, that lands squarely at $10.50, representing an 11% gain from current levels.
Support structure remains solid with the $9.32 level providing immediate downside protection and the $9.20 zone offering secondary support. The lower Bollinger Band at $8.99 represents maximum downside in any correction scenario.
Risk-reward strongly favors bulls. A break above $9.60 opens the path to $10.50 within two weeks, while downside remains limited to the $9.20 support zone. Whales wouldn't position this aggressively without expecting significant upside within their holding timeframes.
The setup is clean, positioning is aggressive, and technicals are aligned. AVAX appears ready to deliver the breakout that compressed volatility has been building toward.

item.title: ALGO Price Prediction: $0.135 Breakout Imminent as Shorts Face Squeeze | ALGO's surge to $0.12 sets up a direct assault on 200-day resistance at $0.13, with 56.6%... https://Blockchain.News/news/20260425-price-prediction-algo-0135-breakout-imminent-as-shorts-...
item.description:
Original title: ALGO Price Prediction: $0.135 Breakout Imminent as Shorts Face Squeeze Summary: ALGO's surge to $0.12 sets up a direct assault on 200-day resistance at $0.13, with 56.6% retail shorts creating explosive upside potential to $0.135. Article text: ALGO's surge to $0.12 sets up a direct assault on 200-day resistance at $0.13, with 56.6% retail shorts creating explosive upside potential to $0.135.
ALGO blasted 8.14% higher to crack $0.12 resistance with $6.68M in volume—real money backing real momentum. The RSI sits at 61.09 with room to run before overbought territory kicks in. Price action shows ALGO testing the upper Bollinger Band at 80% position, pushing the boundaries of recent consolidation.
This isn't a dead cat bounce. The move carries conviction with clear follow-through above yesterday's highs near $0.11. ALGO now trades at the doorstep of its most significant technical hurdle: the 200-day moving average at $0.13.
The 200-day moving average at $0.13 represents the line in the sand for ALGO's medium-term trajectory. Breaking above this level opens a direct path to $0.135, where previous selling pressure emerged.
Short-term moving averages have aligned in ALGO's favor with the 7-day, 12-day EMA, and 26-day EMA clustering around $0.11. This convergence creates a springboard effect—any sustained move above $0.12 triggers rapid acceleration through thin air toward $0.13.
The downside anchor sits at $0.115, where the 20-day SMA and recent bounce zone converge. Breaking below this level puts the 50-day SMA at $0.10 back in play and kills the bullish thesis.
The derivatives market exposes the real story behind ALGO's surge. Retail traders have positioned 56.6% short against 43.4% long—classic squeeze material waiting for a catalyst. Top traders remain cautious at 48.6% long versus 51.4% short, refusing to chase but not betting against momentum either.
Funding rates hold neutral at 0.01% with open interest declining 0.68%, indicating profit-taking rather than fresh short buildup. This creates ideal conditions for a short squeeze if buying pressure sustains above $0.12.
The analysts at Blockchain.news have been tracking ALGO's technical development, and current price action validates the breakout scenario taking shape.
Buy ALGO on any pullback to $0.118-$0.122 with a target of $0.135. Stop-loss goes below $0.115 where support confluence sits. This delivers a clean 2:1 risk-reward setup with multiple technical levels supporting the upside thesis.
The retail short squeeze potential adds explosive upside beyond technical targets if momentum sustains. A break above $0.125 with volume confirmation opens the door to $0.14, though position sizing becomes critical at extended levels.
Hourly candlesticks (about 96 bars), same endpoint as our cryptocurrency price pages. Numbers below refresh from 1-minute klines.
ALGO trades at an inflection point where the next 48 hours determine whether we get a breakout or breakdown. The setup favors bulls, but execution matters.
ALGO's surge to $0.12 sets up a direct assault on 200-day resistance at $0.13, with 56.6% retail shorts creating explosive upside potential to $0.135.
ALGO blasted 8.14% higher to crack $0.12 resistance with $6.68M in volume—real money backing real momentum. The RSI sits at 61.09 with room to run before overbought territory kicks in. Price action shows ALGO testing the upper Bollinger Band at 80% position, pushing the boundaries of recent consolidation.
This isn't a dead cat bounce. The move carries conviction with clear follow-through above yesterday's highs near $0.11. ALGO now trades at the doorstep of its most significant technical hurdle: the 200-day moving average at $0.13.
The 200-day moving average at $0.13 represents the line in the sand for ALGO's medium-term trajectory. Breaking above this level opens a direct path to $0.135, where previous selling pressure emerged.
Short-term moving averages have aligned in ALGO's favor with the 7-day, 12-day EMA, and 26-day EMA clustering around $0.11. This convergence creates a springboard effect—any sustained move above $0.12 triggers rapid acceleration through thin air toward $0.13.
The downside anchor sits at $0.115, where the 20-day SMA and recent bounce zone converge. Breaking below this level puts the 50-day SMA at $0.10 back in play and kills the bullish thesis.
The derivatives market exposes the real story behind ALGO's surge. Retail traders have positioned 56.6% short against 43.4% long—classic squeeze material waiting for a catalyst. Top traders remain cautious at 48.6% long versus 51.4% short, refusing to chase but not betting against momentum either. Source: https://Blockchain.News/news/20260425-price-prediction-algo-0135-breakout-imminent-as-shorts-face

item.description:
Original title: FILE Price Prediction: $1.10 Target Within 14 Days as Technical Breakout Builds Summary: FILE's surge past $0.95 resistance coincides with whale positioning at 65% long ratios, creating a clear path to $1.10 with strong probability. Technical momentum and balanced derivatives markets s Article text: FILE's surge past $0.95 resistance coincides with whale positioning at 65% long ratios, creating a clear path to $1.10 with strong probability. Technical momentum and balanced derivatives markets s...
FILE has established firm control above $0.95, marking a decisive break from its recent consolidation pattern. The token now trades confidently above both its 7-day average at $0.93 and 20-day line at $0.92, creating a technical foundation that institutional buyers continue defending. With RSI at 56, FILE maintains healthy distance from overbought levels while the MACD histogram suggests accumulating momentum beneath current price action.
The Bollinger Band structure shows FILE approaching the upper boundary at $0.99 without displaying typical exhaustion patterns. This positioning, combined with the current 0.73 band ratio, indicates building pressure for an upward resolution rather than a reversal back toward range lows.
Binance spot volume of $4.88 million reflects measured institutional participation rather than speculative retail rushing. The derivatives landscape provides additional insight - open interest declined 2.88% to $34 million over 24 hours, suggesting position cleanup among weaker holders while conviction traders maintained their exposure.
The taker buy/sell ratio at 1.10 demonstrates slight purchasing pressure without excessive imbalance, while the neutral 0.01% funding rate indicates healthy position dynamics. This environment typically precedes sustained moves higher when combined with the current whale positioning at 65% long ratios among top-tier traders.
FILE benefits from broader institutional crypto optimism, with major financial institutions maintaining aggressive digital asset targets that create favorable conditions for quality altcoins. The decentralized storage narrative continues attracting strategic capital allocation as infrastructure plays gain prominence in institutional portfolios.
According to analysts at Blockchain.news, this type of quiet accumulation phase often precedes significant price advancement when technical setups align with smart money positioning. The absence of bearish commentary during FILE's current consolidation mirrors patterns observed before previous breakout sequences.
FILE appears positioned for advancement toward $1.10 within two weeks, representing approximately 16% upside from current levels. This target probability sits near 70% based on converging technical factors and institutional positioning data. Initial resistance at $0.97 should provide temporary friction before the push toward the primary objective.
Risk management centers on the $0.92 support confluence, where the 20-day average intersects with established buying interest. Any breakdown below this threshold would compromise the bullish structure and potentially trigger retesting toward the $0.85 lower Bollinger Band region.
Hourly candlesticks (about 96 bars), same endpoint as our cryptocurrency price pages. Numbers below refresh from 1-minute klines.
The $1.10 target aligns with historical resistance characteristics and offers logical profit-taking opportunities before FILE attempts challenging the more substantial $1.30 barrier represented by longer-term moving average resistance.

item.title: INJ Price Prediction: $4.20 Target Within 10 Days as Whales Double Down | INJ sits at a critical breakout point above $3.58 with smart money positioning aggressive... https://Blockchain.News/news/20260425-price-prediction-target-inj-420-within-10-days-as-whal...
item.description:
Original title: INJ Price Prediction: $4.20 Target Within 10 Days as Whales Double Down Summary: INJ sits at a critical breakout point above $3.58 with smart money positioning aggressively long at 67.5%. The setup screams 70% probability for $4.20+ if bulls crack $3.65, but a rejection here op Article text: INJ sits at a critical breakout point above $3.58 with smart money positioning aggressively long at 67.5%. The setup screams 70% probability for $4.20+ if bulls crack $3.65, but a rejection here op...
INJ just punched through a wall of resistance at $3.58, riding a 3.46% surge that caught bears completely off-guard. The price action is textbook breakout material—trading at the exact Bollinger Band upper limit while momentum indicators flash mixed but leaning bullish signals. With the RSI at 68.13, we're in that sweet spot where momentum is strong but not yet screaming overbought. The MACD histogram sitting flat at zero tells me the previous downtrend has completely stalled, and bulls are now testing whether they can seize control.
What's particularly striking is how INJ carved through its daily range from $3.39 to $3.58—that's a tight compression followed by an explosive move that typically precedes bigger breakouts. The market is clearly deciding right here, right now.
The technical picture reveals a coin perched on a knife's edge. INJ has muscled its way above all short-term moving averages, sitting 7% above the 20-day SMA at $3.17 and 17% above the 50-day at $3.05. But here's the reality check—we're still 27% below the 200-day SMA at $4.90, meaning any sustained rally has massive overhead supply to digest.
The immediate battlefield is crystal clear: $3.65 represents the make-or-break resistance level that bulls must crack to trigger the next leg higher. Below, $3.46 offers the first meaningful support, followed by the more substantial $3.33 level that coincides with recent consolidation zones. The analysts at Blockchain.news see this setup as a classic coiled spring—either we explode higher or collapse back into the $3.00-$3.20 range where buyers previously stepped in.
Here's where it gets interesting—the derivatives market is telling a completely different story than the spot action. While INJ rallied 3.46% today, the funding rate sits at -0.0239%, meaning shorts are actually paying longs in the futures market. This negative funding typically signals bearish sentiment among derivatives traders, creating a contrarian setup.
But dig deeper into the positioning data and the picture shifts dramatically. Top traders—the smart money—are positioned 67.5% long with a 2.08 ratio, while retail follows at 65.6% long. When whales and retail align this heavily on the same side, it often precedes significant moves in that direction. The taker buy/sell ratio at 1.31 shows aggressive buying pressure, with buyers willing to pay market prices rather than wait.
Hourly candlesticks (about 96 bars), same endpoint as our cryptocurrency price pages. Numbers below refresh from 1-minute klines.
With open interest dropping 4.51% to $12.06 million while price rallied, this suggests short covering rather than fresh long accumulation—a healthier foundation for sustained upside.
The setup screams asymmetric opportunity if you can stomach the volatility. I'm targeting $4.20 as the primary upside objective—that's where INJ will hit serious resistance from previous distribution zones and approach the 200-day moving average overhead supply.
Entry zone: $3.55-$3.62 on any pullback or breakout confirmation above $3.65. The stop-loss is non-negotiable at $3.32, just below the strong support cluster where institutional buyers previously emerged. This gives us a tight 7% risk for potential 17% reward—better than 2:1 risk-reward that any serious trader demands.
For the aggressive play, a break above $3.70 with volume could trigger a squeeze toward $4.50, but that's a secondary target requiring sustained momentum. The invalidation is simple: failure to hold $3.32 opens the door to $2.85, where the next major support zone waits.
Timeline probability: 70% chance we test $4.20 within 10 trading days if INJ holds above $3.45. The negative funding rate actually works in bulls' favor here—when sentiment turns, the unwind could be violent to the upside.

item.title: Paul Sztorc to Launch eCash Bitcoin Hard Fork in August | Bitcoin developer Paul Sztorc announces eCash hard fork for August, introducing drivechai... https://Blockchain.News/news/paul-sztorc-ecash-bitcoin-hard-fork
item.description:
Original title: Paul Sztorc to Launch eCash Bitcoin Hard Fork in August Summary: Bitcoin developer Paul Sztorc announces eCash hard fork for August, introducing drivechains and controversy over Satoshi's coins. Article text: Bitcoin developer Paul Sztorc announces eCash hard fork for August, introducing drivechains and controversy over Satoshi's coins.
Paul Sztorc, a well-known Bitcoin developer and creator of Drivechain, has announced plans to launch a new Bitcoin hard fork called eCash in August 2026. The fork will allow Bitcoin holders to exchange BTC for eCash on a 1:1 basis, marking a significant step in Sztorc’s long-standing efforts to expand Bitcoin's scalability and functionality.
According to Sztorc, eCash will run on a layer-1 blockchain that closely mirrors Bitcoin Core software and utilizes the same SHA-256 hashing algorithm. One key modification is a reduction in initial mining difficulty, intended to make it easier for miners to participate. Additionally, the eCash network will include seven layer-2 scaling solutions, known as “drivechains,” designed to increase transaction throughput and offer optional on-chain privacy.
What has sparked the most heated debate is Sztorc’s proposal to “manually” reassign a portion of Satoshi Nakamoto’s 1.1 million BTC – coins believed to be inaccessible – to early investors. Sztorc argues the move is a “sustainable fix to Bitcoin's problems,” but prominent Bitcoin advocates have expressed strong opposition. Peter McCormack, a vocal Bitcoin supporter, criticized the plan, calling it “theft and disrespectful.” Similarly, other critics have questioned the need for yet another Bitcoin fork.
Sztorc has sought to distance eCash from past Bitcoin forks like Bitcoin Cash (BCH), which launched in 2017 but failed to achieve significant adoption. “Unlike BCH, there is no ‘Bitcoin’ in the name here,” Sztorc said, emphasizing that eCash aims to address Bitcoin’s technical limitations without creating confusion with the original chain.
The announcement comes at a time when Bitcoin is trading at $77,638 with modest daily gains of 0.44%. The broader market has seen cooling volatility, signaling a slowdown in momentum after recent highs. Sztorc’s timing could reignite debate over Bitcoin’s scalability and privacy as it competes with increasingly advanced blockchain ecosystems like Ethereum and Solana.
Sztorc has been a prominent figure in Bitcoin development circles for over a decade. He is best known for his proposal of BIP 300 and BIP 301, which introduced the Drivechain concept—an approach to integrating sidechains into Bitcoin. This fork represents a culmination of his efforts to push the boundaries of Bitcoin’s capabilities while addressing long-standing criticisms around scalability.
If eCash progresses as planned, it will go live in August, providing Bitcoin holders the option to test the new network. However, the controversial reallocation of Satoshi's coins and the mixed reception from the Bitcoin community could pose challenges for adoption. Traders and developers will be watching closely to see whether eCash can deliver on its promises or face the same fate as past forks like BCH.
Bitcoin developer Paul Sztorc announces eCash hard fork for August, introducing drivechains and controversy over Satoshi's coins.

According to Sztorc, eCash will run on a layer-1 blockchain that closely mirrors Bitcoin Core software and utilizes the same SHA-256 hashing algorithm. One key modification is a reduction in initial mining difficulty, intended to make it easier for miners to participate. Additionally, the eCash network will include seven layer-2 scaling solutions, known as “drivechains,” designed to increase transaction throughput and offer optional on-chain privacy.
What has sparked the most heated debate is Sztorc’s proposal to “manually” reassign a portion of Satoshi Nakamoto’s 1.1 million BTC – coins believed to be inaccessible – to early investors. Sztorc argues the move is a “sustainable fix to Bitcoin's problems,” but prominent Bitcoin advocates have expressed strong opposition. Peter McCormack, a vocal Bitcoin supporter, criticized the plan, calling it “theft and disrespectful.” Similarly, other critics have questioned the need for yet another Bitcoin fork.
Sztorc has sought to distance eCash from past Bitcoin forks like Bitcoin Cash (BCH), which launched in 2017 but failed to achieve significant adoption. “Unlike BCH, there is no ‘Bitcoin’ in the name here,” Sztorc said, emphasizing that eCash aims to address Bitcoin’s technical limitations without creating confusion with the original chain.
The announcement comes at a time when Bitcoin is trading at $77,638 with modest daily gains of 0.44%. The broader market has seen cooling volatility, signaling a slowdown in momentum after recent highs. Sztorc’s timing could reignite debate over Bitcoin’s scalability and privacy as it competes with increasingly advanced blockchain ecosystems like Ethereum and Solana.
Sztorc has been a prominent figure in Bitcoin development circles for over a decade. He is best known for his proposal of BIP 300 and BIP 301, which introduced the Drivechain concept—an approach to integrating sidechains into Bitcoin. This fork represents a culmination of his efforts to push the boundaries of Bitcoin’s capabilities while addressing long-standing criticisms around scalability.
If eCash progresses as planned, it will go live in August, providing Bitcoin holders the option to test the new network. However, the controversial reallocation of Satoshi's coins and the mixed reception from the Bitcoin community could pose challenges for adoption. Traders and developers will be watching closely to see whether eCash can deliver on its promises or face the same fate as past forks like BCH.

item.content:encoded:
Bitcoin developer Paul Sztorc announces eCash hard fork for August, introducing drivechains and controversy over Satoshi's coins.
Paul Sztorc, a well-known Bitcoin developer and creator of Drivechain, has announced plans to launch a new Bitcoin hard fork called eCash in August 2026. The fork will allow Bitcoin holders to exchange BTC for eCash on a 1:1 basis, marking a significant step in Sztorc’s long-standing efforts to expand Bitcoin's scalability and functionality.
According to Sztorc, eCash will run on a layer-1 blockchain that closely mirrors Bitcoin Core software and utilizes the same SHA-256 hashing algorithm. One key modification is a reduction in initial mining difficulty, intended to make it easier for miners to participate. Additionally, the eCash network will include seven layer-2 scaling solutions, known as “drivechains,” designed to increase transaction throughput and offer optional on-chain privacy.
What has sparked the most heated debate is Sztorc’s proposal to “manually” reassign a portion of Satoshi Nakamoto’s 1.1 million BTC – coins believed to be inaccessible – to early investors. Sztorc argues the move is a “sustainable fix to Bitcoin's problems,” but prominent Bitcoin advocates have expressed strong opposition. Peter McCormack, a vocal Bitcoin supporter, criticized the plan, calling it “theft and disrespectful.” Similarly, other critics have questioned the need for yet another Bitcoin fork.
Sztorc has sought to distance eCash from past Bitcoin forks like Bitcoin Cash (BCH), which launched in 2017 but failed to achieve significant adoption. “Unlike BCH, there is no ‘Bitcoin’ in the name here,” Sztorc said, emphasizing that eCash aims to address Bitcoin’s technical limitations without creating confusion with the original chain.
The announcement comes at a time when Bitcoin is trading at $77,638 with modest daily gains of 0.44%. The broader market has seen cooling volatility, signaling a slowdown in momentum after recent highs. Sztorc’s timing could reignite debate over Bitcoin’s scalability and privacy as it competes with increasingly advanced blockchain ecosystems like Ethereum and Solana.
Sztorc has been a prominent figure in Bitcoin development circles for over a decade. He is best known for his proposal of BIP 300 and BIP 301, which introduced the Drivechain concept—an approach to integrating sidechains into Bitcoin. If eCash progresses as planned, it will go live in August, providing Bitcoin holders the option to test the new network. However, the controversial reallocation of Satoshi's coins and the mixed reception from the Bitcoin community could pose challenges for adoption. Traders and developers will be watching closely to see whether eCash can deliver on its promises or face the same fate as past forks like BCH.

item.title: US Freezes $344M in Crypto Linked to Iran's IRGC, Hizballah | US Treasury freezes $344M in Iran-linked crypto wallets tied to the IRGC and Hizballah, p... https://Blockchain.News/news/us-freezes-crypto-iran-irgc-hizballah
item.description:
Original title: US Freezes $344M in Crypto Linked to Iran's IRGC, Hizballah Summary: US Treasury freezes $344M in Iran-linked crypto wallets tied to the IRGC and Hizballah, part of broader sanctions targeting Tehran's financial networks. Article text: US Treasury freezes $344M in Iran-linked crypto wallets tied to the IRGC and Hizballah, part of broader sanctions targeting Tehran's financial networks.
The U.S. Treasury has frozen $344 million in cryptocurrency allegedly tied to Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) and Hizballah, according to an April 24 announcement. The action, carried out through the Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC), sanctioned specific crypto wallets on the Tron blockchain, marking another escalation in the U.S.'s financial pressure campaign against Tehran.
Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent stated via social media that the effort is part of a broader strategy to "systematically degrade Tehran’s ability to generate, move, and repatriate funds." This freeze also follows recent reports of Iran using Bitcoin (BTC) to charge tolls for ships passing through the Strait of Hormuz, an oil shipping chokepoint, further illustrating how digital assets are becoming a tool in geopolitical conflicts.
The sanctions are the latest in a series of U.S. actions targeting Iranian-linked crypto networks. Back in January 2026, OFAC sanctioned two UK-registered exchanges, Zedcex and Zedxion, for processing billions in transactions allegedly tied to the IRGC. These sanctions marked a shift from targeting individual wallets to blacklisting entire exchange platforms suspected of facilitating illicit activity.
Friday’s freeze also involved stablecoin issuer Tether, which confirmed it had acted at the request of U.S. authorities to block $344 million in USDT transactions. Although Tether’s initial statement didn’t explicitly name Iran, it aligns with the Treasury's broader strategy, informally referred to as 'Economic Fury,' to disrupt Iran's financial operations globally.
The use of cryptocurrencies by sanctioned entities like the IRGC highlights why regulators are increasingly scrutinizing the role of digital assets in evading traditional financial controls. Unlike hawala networks or gold, crypto offers programmability and cross-border fluidity, making it a preferred choice for circumventing sanctions.
Blockchain analytics firms, such as Chainalysis and Elliptic, continue to play a critical role in tracing the movement of these funds. Their tools allow authorities to identify wallet clusters and map complex financial flows, enabling targeted actions like Friday’s freeze. For crypto investors, such measures underscore the importance of compliance and due diligence, especially when operating on platforms with lax controls.
The timing of the sanctions is notable. It comes amid heightened tensions in the Strait of Hormuz, where Iran has reportedly begun collecting tolls from ships in Bitcoin. Combined with recent U.S.-Israeli airstrikes on Iranian targets, this paints a picture of escalating financial and military conflicts. While U.S. officials announced a ceasefire agreement earlier this week, the region remains volatile, with reports of Iranian attacks on maritime vessels and U.S. naval blockades in place.
For market participants, these developments could have broader implications for stablecoin liquidity and the reputation of blockchain networks like Tron, which hosted the sanctioned wallets. Any perception of increased regulatory risk may prompt traders to reassess their exposure to these assets and platforms.
With these latest sanctions, the U.S. has demonstrated its commitment to expanding enforcement into the crypto space. As Iran appears to be leaning further into digital assets as a workaround for sanctions, expect OFAC and other agencies to ramp up blockchain analytics and enforcement actions. For traders, staying ahead of regulatory developments is crucial, especially as geopolitical tensions continue to impact both the crypto markets and broader financial systems.

item.title: Core Scientific (CORZ) Sets Q1 2026 Earnings Call for May 6 | Core Scientific will release Q1 2026 earnings on May 6, amid its pivot to AI infrastructu... https://Blockchain.News/news/core-scientific-q1-2026-earnings-call
item.description:
Original title: Core Scientific (CORZ) Sets Q1 2026 Earnings Call for May 6 Summary: Core Scientific will release Q1 2026 earnings on May 6, amid its pivot to AI infrastructure. Key details on webcast and investor context. Article text: Core Scientific will release Q1 2026 earnings on May 6, amid its pivot to AI infrastructure. Key details on webcast and investor context.
Core Scientific, Inc. (Nasdaq: CORZ), a leading provider of digital infrastructure and high-density colocation (HDC) services, has scheduled its first quarter 2026 earnings release for May 6, 2026. The financial results will be available after market close, followed by an investor conference call and webcast at 3:30 p.m. CT.
The event will feature Core Scientific’s executive team, including CEO Adam Sullivan, COO Matt Brown, CFO Jim Nygaard, and VP of Investor Relations Jon Charbonneau, who will provide an in-depth review of the company's performance and strategy. Investors can access the live webcast on the company's official investor relations page, with an audio replay available post-event.
This earnings call comes as Core Scientific continues its significant pivot away from Bitcoin mining to focus on building GPU-powered data centers tailored for AI and other high-performance computing applications. The company has been aggressively repurposing its facilities to support HDC services, a move underscored by its recent $3.3 billion senior secured notes offering to fund data center expansion.
Core Scientific’s transformation has been closely watched by investors. Despite reporting $319.02 million in revenue for 2025—a decline from the previous year—the company has retained strong analyst support with a 'Strong Buy' rating and a 12-month price target of $24.56. The stock was trading at $21.02 as of April 23, 2026, giving the company a market cap of $6.66 billion.
However, not all investor sentiment is bullish. On April 23, Two Seas Capital disclosed it had reduced its stake in Core Scientific to 4.4%, signaling some hesitation about the company’s ongoing transformation.
The transition away from Bitcoin mining has been costly but strategic. Core Scientific sold approximately $175 million worth of Bitcoin in March 2026 to accelerate its AI infrastructure investments. The company has also announced plans to fully discontinue Bitcoin mining operations, aiming to capitalize on the growing demand for AI-driven computational services.
Investors will be keen to see whether the first quarter results reflect meaningful progress in this direction. The earnings call on May 6 could provide clarity on the company's revenue composition, margins from HDC services, and the execution of its expansion plans.
For shareholders and analysts, the key questions remain: Can Core Scientific successfully shift its business model to capitalize on AI infrastructure demand? And will this pivot translate into sustainable profitability? Answers may begin to emerge during the upcoming earnings release and investor call.
